Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Design, setting, and participants' section on page 1 states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. This indicates two treatment arms: ADT plus D and ADT alone. This is further confirmed on page 2 under '2. Patients and methods': '385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (...) or ADT alone'.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Design, setting, and participants' section of the Abstract, it states: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'. This is confirmed on page 2 under 'Patients and methods' and on page 3 under 'Results', both stating '385 patients were enrolled'.

Treatment Arm(s).value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel; Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Alone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The title of the paper states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section in the abstract further confirms this: 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients'.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: In the 'Patients and methods' section on page 2, it states: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The 'ADT plus D' arm is the experimental arm.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Hormonal therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy) and Chemotherapy (Docetaxel)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The title of the paper states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section on page 1 and '2. Patients and methods' section on page 2 confirm that patients were randomized to 'ADT plus D' or 'ADT alone'. ADT is described as 'orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors', which are hormonal therapies. Docetaxel is a chemotherapy agent.
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: The 'Abstract' on page 1 states: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo, median OS in the overall population was 62.1 mo'. Additionally, in section '3.1. Overall survival' on page 3, it says: 'After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo (95% CI, 82.9-84.7), 242 patients had died'. Table 2 on page 4 also lists 'Median follow-up, mo' as '83.9' for GETUG-AFU 15.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 2, under '2. Patients and methods', it states: 'From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The ADT arm is the control arm.

Control Arm.evidence: The title of the paper states "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone". The 'Design, setting, and participants' section on page 1 explicitly mentions: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients". The '2. Patients and methods' section on page 2 further clarifies: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone." The '3. Results' section on page 3 consistently refers to the comparison group as the "ADT arm" (e.g., "193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm").

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) plus Docetaxel (D) (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles). ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: Found in the 'Patients and methods' section on page 2, which describes the randomization of eligible patients into two arms. The first arm is specified as 'ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles)' and further details that 'ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.'

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival, progression-free survival, and metastatic burden. There is no mention of a 'Quality of Life Scale' or any assessment of participants' overall well-being and satisfaction with their health status in the context of the GETUG-AFU15 trial. Reference [26] mentions 'quality of life' in the context of a different trial (GETUG 12), but this is not a finding from the current study.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document describes the GETUG-AFU15 trial and its outcomes, focusing on overall survival, biochemical progression-free survival, and radiographic progression-free survival. There is no mention or reporting of results related to the quality of life of participants in the main text, abstract, or results sections. While a reference (number 26) mentions 'quality of life' in the context of a different trial (GETUG 12), it does not indicate that QoL was reported for the GETUG-AFU15 trial discussed in this paper.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document reports 'Median OS' and 'Median Progression-Free Survival' for the treatment arm (ADT plus D), but does not explicitly state the 'Median On-Treatment Duration (mo)' for this arm.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The document reports 'Median OS' and 'Median Progression-Free Survival' for the control arm (ADT alone), but does not explicitly state the 'Median On-Treatment Duration (mo)' for this arm. It mentions 'salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo' in the ADT arm, but this refers to subsequent treatment, not the initial on-treatment duration for the control therapy itself.

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly mentioned in the title of the article: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial".

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)', under 'Age, yr, median (IQR)' for the 'ADT (n = 193)' column on page 3.

NCT.evidence: Found on page 3, in the "3. Results" section, stating "The trial is registered with ClinicalTrials.gov (NCT00104715)."

PubMed ID.evidence: A PubMed ID for this specific publication is not explicitly reported in the document.

Trial Name.evidence: Extracted from the main title of the article on page 1: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer: Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial".

Author.evidence: Found on page 1, under the title, listing "Gwenaelle Gravis et al." as the authors.

Year.evidence: Found on page 1, at the top of the page, indicating "EUROPEAN UROLOGY 70 (2016)".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document contains multiple sections (Introduction, Methods, Results, Discussion, Conclusions, References) and spans several pages, indicating it is a full publication rather than just an abstract.

Phase.evidence: Found on page 1, in the main title and the abstract, explicitly stating "Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title mentions "Long-term Survival Analysis" and the introduction on page 2 states "we assessed the benefits... and previously showed that OS was not different... and updated survival analyses. We present long-term outcomes...", indicating this is a follow-up analysis of an existing trial.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Patients and methods' section, it states that '385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm. Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.' The 'ADT plus D arm' is the treatment arm, and all 192 patients in this arm were randomized to receive docetaxel as part of their treatment.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 2, in the 'Patients and methods' section, it states that '385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.' The 'ADT arm' is the control arm with 193 patients. On page 5, in the 'Discussion' section, it is stated that 'In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179) indicating that this trial was more a trial of early versus delayed D.' Therefore, 79% of 193 patients (approximately 152 patients) in the control arm received docetaxel as salvage therapy during the trial.

PS - N (%) | 0 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1 'Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)' on page 3, under 'ECOG performance status, n (%)' row '0', column 'ADT plus D (n = 192)'.

Primary Endpoint(s).evidence: The 'Abstract' section on page 1, under 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis', explicitly states: 'Primary end point was OS.'

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progres-sion-free survival (rPFS)
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: The 'Abstract' section on page 1, under 'Outcome measurements and statistical analysis', explicitly states: 'Secondary end points were biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progres-sion-free survival (rPFS).'

Gleason score - N (%) | ≤ 7 | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Gleason score, n (%)', sub-row '<7', under the 'ADT plus D (n = 192)' column (Treatment arm).

Gleason score - N (%) | ≥ 8 | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, row 'Gleason score, n (%)', sub-row '>8', under the 'ADT (n = 193)' column (Control arm).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The document reports results for 'patients with metastases at diagnosis' on page 2, which refers to synchronous metastases. For example, 'For upfront metastatic patients, OS was 52.6 mo (95% CI, 43.3-66.8) and 41.5 mo (95% CI, 36.3-54.5), respectively (HR: 0.93 [95% CI, 0.69-1.25]; p = 0.6).' Also, on page 5, it mentions 'patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases', where 'de novo' refers to synchronous.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document reports results for 'patients with metastases after failure of local treatment' on page 2, which refers to metachronous diseases. For example, 'For patients with metastases after failure of local treatment, median OS was not reached (95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and was 69.8 mo (95% CI, 62.2-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.83 [95% CI, 0.47-1.47]; p = 0.5) (Supplementary Table 2).' Also, on page 5, it mentions 'patients with de novo and secondary metastatic diseases', where 'secondary metastatic diseases' refers to metachronous.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The Abstract on page 1 states the objective is 'To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.' On page 2, it explicitly states, 'We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups.' HVD is defined as 'high-volume disease'. Results for HVD are presented, for example, on page 3: 'In patients with HVD, median OS was 4.7 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm, but the difference was not statistically significant: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0-53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9-43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14) (Fig. 2).'

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The Abstract on page 1 states the objective is 'To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.' On page 2, it explicitly states, 'We present long-term outcomes in the overall population and in the HVD and LVD subgroups.' LVD is defined as 'low-volume disease'. Results for LVD are presented, for example, on page 3: 'In patients with LVD, median OS was not reached (NR; 95% CI, 69.5-NR) in the ADT plus D arm and 83.4 mo (95% CI, 61.8-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 1.02 [95% CI, 0.67–1.55]; p = 0.9) (Fig. 3).'

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the paper is "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone...". The abstract's 'Design, setting, and participants' section states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone...". The introduction also mentions "the addition of D to ADT". 'D' is an abbreviation for Docetaxel, as seen in the Keywords section.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: The document focuses on overall survival, biochemical progression-free survival, and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not provide a breakdown of all-cause adverse events by grade for the treatment arm.

The document focuses on overall survival, biochemical progression-free survival, and radiographic progression-free survival. It does not provide a breakdown of all-cause adverse events by grade for the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm' on page 4. Deaths are Grade 5 events, which are a subset of Grade 3 or higher. However, it only provides the count for Grade 5 treatment-related events, not for all Grade 3 or higher treatment-related events.

The document mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm' (treatment arm) on page 4. There is no information provided for treatment-related adverse events of Grade 3 or higher in the control arm (ADT alone).

On page 4, under section 3.2, it states: 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm'. Deaths are considered Grade 5 adverse events. Table 1 on page 3 indicates that N=192 patients were in the ADT plus D (treatment) arm. Therefore, 4/192 = 2.08%.

The document mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm' (treatment arm) on page 4. There is no information provided for treatment-related adverse events of Grade 5 in the control arm (ADT alone).

TTPSA (mo) | Treatment.evidence: On page 4, in section 3.2 'Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival', it states: 'The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo'. bPFS (biochemical progression-free survival) is defined based on PSA progression, making it equivalent to TTPSA. The ADT plus D arm is the treatment arm.

TTPSA (mo) | Control.evidence: On page 4, in section 3.2 'Biochemical and radiographic progression-free survival', it states: '...versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7)'. This value corresponds to the ADT arm, which is the control arm. bPFS (biochemical progression-free survival) is defined based on PSA progression, making it equivalent to TTPSA.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document is a report of a single randomized controlled trial (RCT) and discusses its own results, limitations (e.g., lack of statistical power, retrospective analysis), and comparisons with other RCTs. However, it does not provide an explicit 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence (COE)' in a standardized format or framework (like GRADE) across multiple RCTs or even for its own evidence in a summarized, extractable form as requested by the label.

ORR - N (%) | Control | PD.evidence: The document focuses on Overall Survival (OS), biochemical Progression-Free Survival (bPFS), and radiographic Progression-Free Survival (rPFS). While 'radiographic progression' is mentioned, the percentage of patients showing Progression of Disease (PD) as an initial response to treatment for the control arm (ADT alone) is not reported.

Type of Therapy.value: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel (D) versus ADT Alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors. Docetaxel was administered at 75 mg/m² for 21 days, up to nine cycles.
Type of Therapy.evidence: The title of the paper states 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone'. The 'Design, setting, and participants' section in the Abstract further specifies 'Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone'. The 'Patients and methods' section on page 2 provides more detail: 'Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors.' I combined these details to form the description of the therapy.

Treatment Class.evidence: The clinical trial compares two treatment arms: 'Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel' and 'ADT Alone' (Page 1, Title and Abstract). Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) is a form of hormone therapy, and Docetaxel is a chemotherapy drug. Therefore, the treatments received by participants fall under the classes of Hormone Therapy and Chemotherapy. This classification is further supported by references to similar trials (e.g., CHAARTED trial, cited on page 5), which are described as comparing 'chemohormonal therapy versus hormonal therapy' (References [14] and [15]).

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The document reports median Progression-Free Survival (PFS) in months (e.g., 22.9 months for ADT plus D arm for bPFS and rPFS in the overall population on page 4, section 3.2), but does not report a PFS rate as a percentage. The hazard ratio is a comparative measure between arms, not a rate for a single arm.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The document reports median Progression-Free Survival (PFS) in months (e.g., 12.9 months for ADT arm for bPFS and 15.3 months for rPFS in the overall population on page 4, section 3.2), but does not report a PFS rate as a percentage. The hazard ratio is a comparative measure between arms, not a rate for a single arm.
